name: Ascent to San Millan and Cabeza Aguilez — circular through the Sierra de la Demanda Natural Space
name_original: Subida a San Millán y a Cabeza Aguiléz — circular por el Espacio Natural de la Sierra de la Demanda
region_intro: Hiking around Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión offers access to the varied terrain of the Sierra de la Demanda mountain range in Burgos, Spain. The region features significant peaks like Pico San Millán, lush river valleys carved by the Urbión River, and well-preserved beech forests. Hikers can explore diverse landscapes, from challenging mountain ascents to scenic paths alongside waterfalls such as those found along the Altuzarra stream. Best hiking trails around Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión The most popular hiking route is Senda de las Cascadas — circular by the Espacio Natural de la Sierra de la Demanda, a 4.3 miles (7.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 29 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads through leafy beech forests to spectacular cascades. Another top favourite among local hikers is High Route through Covanegra — circular via Garganchon Gorge, a moderate 7.4 miles (11.9 km) path. This trail explores the Garganchon Gorge, offering varied terrain and views. Local hikers also love the Ascent to San Millan and Cabeza Aguilez — circular through the Sierra de la Demanda Natural Space, a 11.5 miles (18.6 km) trail leading through the Sierra de la Demanda Natural Space, often completed in about 6 hours 58 minutes. Hiking around Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión is defined by the Sierra de la Demanda mountain range, Altuzarra Waterfalls, and extensive beech forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate forest paths to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión?
A: There are over 45 hiking routes in Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a diverse range of paths, from moderate forest walks to more challenging mountain ascents within the Sierra de la Demanda.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking around Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión?
A: The terrain around Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión is highly varied, reflecting its location in the Sierra de la Demanda mountain range. You can expect mountainous landscapes, lush river valleys carved by the Urbión River, and extensive, well-preserved beech forests. Some routes feature significant elevation changes, while others offer more gentle riverside paths.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión?
A: Yes, Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión offers several easy hiking options suitable for families. There are 7 easy routes available, often following riverside paths through beautiful forests. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041602606/senda-de-las-cascadas-circular-by-the-espacio-natural-de-la-sierra-de-la-demanda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de las Cascadas&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is a moderate route that can be enjoyed by many, leading through enchanting beech forests to spectacular cascades.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión?
A: Yes, many of the hiking routes in Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041602606/senda-de-las-cascadas-circular-by-the-espacio-natural-de-la-sierra-de-la-demanda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de las Cascadas&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041629962/high-route-through-covanegra-circular-via-garganchon-gorge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;High Route through Covanegra&lt;/a&gt;, both offering diverse scenery in a loop.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls in Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión?
A: Absolutely! The region is famous for its waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041602606/senda-de-las-cascadas-circular-by-the-espacio-natural-de-la-sierra-de-la-demanda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de las Cascadas&lt;/a&gt; (Path of the Altuzarra Waterfalls) is a highlight, leading through a leafy beech forest to impressive cascades like the "Small Jump," "Middle Jump," and "Double Jump" along the Altuzarra stream.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I discover on hikes in this region?
A: Hikers can discover several notable natural landmarks and viewpoints. The highest peak in Burgos, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4214289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Millán (2,131 m)&lt;/a&gt;, offers challenging ascents and panoramic views. Other points of interest include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5278503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz De Guirnaldo&lt;/a&gt; and the Viewpoint of Domingo Sevilla, which provides magnificent vistas of the Burgos Sierra de la Demanda.

Q: Are there any notable forests to explore while hiking?
A: Yes, the region is renowned for its magnificent and well-preserved beech forests, such as the Hayedo del río Urbión. These forests are particularly stunning in autumn when the foliage changes color. You can also find rowan, holly, and ancient yew trees within these areas. The Senda "Enrique del Rivero" specifically highlights a famous beech tree in the Urbión river's beech forest.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: For experienced trekkers, Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión offers challenging routes, particularly those ascending to higher peaks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041620202/ascent-to-san-millan-and-cabeza-aguilez-circular-through-the-sierra-de-la-demanda-natural-space" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to San Millan and Cabeza Aguilez&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.5-mile (18.6 km) trail with significant elevation gain, providing a rewarding experience through the Sierra de la Demanda Natural Space.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from challenging mountain ascents to scenic paths alongside waterfalls, and the beauty of the extensive beech forests. The well-marked trails and the natural tranquility are also frequently highlighted.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión?
A: The best time to hike in Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión is generally during spring, summer, and autumn. Spring brings lush greenery and blooming wildflowers, while summer offers warm, sunny days. Autumn is particularly spectacular due to the vibrant colors of the beech forests. While winter hiking is possible, higher elevations may require snowshoes or specific gear due to snow and colder conditions.

Q: Are there any long-distance trails that pass through Santa Cruz Del Valle Urbión?
A: Yes, the GR-82 La Demanda Mountain Range, a long-distance circular path, passes through Santa Cruz del Valle Urbión. This route covers nearly 300 km and connects 36 towns in Burgos, offering extensive trekking opportunities for those looking for multi-day adventures.
tips: At different times of the year it has different attractions

The San Millán or Torruco peak is one of the giants of the Sierra de la Demanda, it has an altitude of 2,131 meters and is the geographical ceiling of the province of Burgos. The relative smoothness of the southern slopes facilitate the possibility of designing different trail running itineraries to the pingorota at the top, which has splendid views and is always comforting after the endless climb from Pineda de la Sierra.
One of the summits of the Cordal de la Demanda
